headlineSummary: Sentiment across Microsoft Copilot, Anthropic, Google Gemini, and OpenAI is tightly clustered in a close tie, with Anthropic getting mixed reactions while the others lean negative on limited evidence [E1][E2][E3].

ranking.summary: Microsoft Copilot draws a negative comment about confusing branding and weak user guidance, but this rests on limited evidence [E3].
Anthropic gets one strong criticism of Claude/Opus quality and reliability alongside one positive comment praising its API/token policy, leaving a mixed but slightly balanced read [E1][E2].
Google Gemini is mentioned only as another marketed alternative in a negative comparison, so the signal is negative but based on limited evidence [E1].
OpenAI is only mentioned alongside Gemini as another overhyped alternative, giving it a mildly negative signal on limited evidence [E1].

evidence.excerpt: Dealing with Claude going into stupid mode 15 times a day, constant HTTP errors, etc. just isn't really worth it for all it does. I can't see myself justifying $200/mo. on any replacement tool either, the output just doesn't warrant it. I think we all jumped on the AI mothership with our eyes closed and it's time to dial some nuance back into things. Most of the time I'm just using Opus as a bulk code autocomplete that really doesn't take much smarts comparatively speaking. But when I do lean on it for actual fiddly bug fixing or ideation, I'm regularly left disappointed and working by hand anyway. I'd prefer to set my expectations (and willingness to pay) a little lower just to get a consistent slightly dumb agent rather than an overpriced one that continually lets me down. I don't think that's a problem fixed by trying to swap in another heavily marketed cure-all like Gemini or Codex, it's solved by adjusting expectations.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names Claude and Opus and strongly criticizes them as going into 'stupid mode', having 'constant HTTP errors', and 'continually lets me down'.
Gemini is explicitly named as 'another heavily marketed cure-all', a mild negative characterization focused on hype/positioning.
Codex is explicitly named and grouped with Gemini as 'another heavily marketed cure-all', indicating mild criticism.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic is actually making the right decision: You want a LOT of tokens for your 24/7 agent? Ok, just use the API and pay for your tokens.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says 'Anthropic is actually making the right decision', endorsing Anthropic's access/policy choice.

evidence.excerpt: If someone says "I used Copilot to..." or "Copilot is great for..." or "Copilot sucks because..." they haven't communicated any useful information to me, because I have no idea what product they are talking about. And if I ask them (which I always do) they still have trouble describing the product, because Microsoft give them no help at all.

annotations.rationale: In the Microsoft Copilot story context, the commenter says Copilot naming communicates no useful information and that Microsoft gives users 'no help at all', indicating negative sentiment about the product branding/strategy.
